NKJV.1: A soft answer turns away wrath, But a harsh word stirs up anger.
NKJV.2: The tongue of the wise uses knowledge rightly,  But the mouth of fools pours forth foolishness.
NKJV.3: The eyes of the LORD are in every place, Keeping watch on the evil and the good.
NKJV.4: A wholesome tongue is a tree of life, But perverseness in it breaks the spirit.
NKJV.5: A fool despises his father’s instruction,  But he who receives correction is prudent.
NKJV.6: In the house of the righteous there is much treasure, But in the revenue of the wicked is trouble.
NKJV.7: The lips of the wise disperse knowledge, But the heart of the fool does not do so.
NKJV.8: The sacrifice of the wicked is an abomination to the LORD, But the prayer of the upright is His delight.
NKJV.9: The way of the wicked is an abomination to the LORD, But He loves him who follows righteousness.
NKJV.10: Harsh discipline is for him who forsakes the way, And he who hates correction will die.
NKJV.11: Hell and Destruction are before the LORD; So how much more the hearts of the sons of men.
NKJV.12: A scoffer does not love one who corrects him, Nor will he go to the wise.
NKJV.13: A merry heart makes a cheerful countenance, But by sorrow of the heart the spirit is broken.
NKJV.14: The heart of him who has understanding seeks knowledge, But the mouth of fools feeds on foolishness.
NKJV.15: All the days of the afflicted are evil, But he who is of a merry heart has a continual feast.
NKJV.16: Better is a little with the fear of the LORD, Than great treasure with trouble.
NKJV.17: Better is a dinner of herbs where love is, Than a fatted calf with hatred.
NKJV.18: A wrathful man stirs up strife, But he who is slow to anger allays contention.
NKJV.19: The way of the lazy man is like a hedge of thorns, But the way of the upright is a highway.
NKJV.20: A wise son makes a father glad, But a foolish man despises his mother.
NKJV.21: Folly is joy to him who is destitute of discernment, But a man of understanding walks uprightly.
NKJV.22: Without counsel, plans go awry, But in the multitude of counselors they are established.
NKJV.23: A man has joy by the answer of his mouth, And a word spoken in due season, how good it is!
NKJV.24: The way of life winds upward for the wise, That he may turn away from hell below.
NKJV.25: The LORD will destroy the house of the proud, But He will establish the boundary of the widow.
NKJV.26: The thoughts of the wicked are an abomination to the LORD, But the words of the pure are pleasant.
NKJV.27: He who is greedy for gain troubles his own house, But he who hates bribes will live.
NKJV.28: The heart of the righteous studies how to answer, But the mouth of the wicked pours forth evil.
NKJV.29: The LORD is far from the wicked, But He hears the prayer of the righteous.
NKJV.30: The light of the eyes rejoices the heart, And a good report makes the bones healthy.
NKJV.31: The ear that hears the rebukes of life Will abide among the wise.
NKJV.32: He who disdains instruction despises his own soul, But he who heeds rebuke gets understanding.
NKJV.33: The fear of the LORD is the instruction of wisdom, And before honor is humility.

ESV.1: A soft answer turns away wrath, but a harsh word stirs up anger.
ESV.2: The tongue of the wise commends knowledge, but the mouths of fools pour out folly.
ESV.3: The eyes of the LORD are in every place, keeping watch on the evil and the good.
ESV.4: A gentle  tongue is a tree of life, but perverseness in it breaks the spirit.
ESV.5: A fool despises his father's instruction, but whoever heeds reproof is prudent.
ESV.6: In the house of the righteous there is much treasure, but trouble befalls the income of the wicked.
ESV.7: The lips of the wise spread knowledge; not so the hearts of fools.
ESV.8: The sacrifice of the wicked is an abomination to the LORD, but the prayer of the upright is acceptable to him.
ESV.9: The way of the wicked is an abomination to the LORD, but he loves him who pursues righteousness.
ESV.10: There is severe discipline for him who forsakes the way; whoever hates reproof will die.
ESV.11: Sheol and Abaddon lie open before the LORD; how much more the hearts of the children of man!
ESV.12: A scoffer does not like to be reproved; he will not go to the wise.
ESV.13: A glad heart makes a cheerful face, but by sorrow of heart the spirit is crushed.
ESV.14: The heart of him who has understanding seeks knowledge, but the mouths of fools feed on folly.
ESV.15: All the days of the afflicted are evil, but the cheerful of heart has a continual feast.
ESV.16: Better is a little with the fear of the LORD than great treasure and trouble with it.
ESV.17: Better is a dinner of herbs where love is than a fattened ox and hatred with it.
ESV.18: A hot-tempered man stirs up strife, but he who is slow to anger quiets contention.
ESV.19: The way of a sluggard is like a hedge of thorns, but the path of the upright is a level highway.
ESV.20: A wise son makes a glad father, but a foolish man despises his mother.
ESV.21: Folly is a joy to him who lacks sense, but a man of understanding walks straight ahead.
ESV.22: Without counsel plans fail, but with many advisers they succeed.
ESV.23: To make an apt answer is a joy to a man, and a word in season, how good it is!
ESV.24: The path of life leads upward for the prudent, that he may turn away from Sheol beneath.
ESV.25: The LORD tears down the house of the proud but maintains the widow's boundaries.
ESV.26: The thoughts of the wicked are an abomination to the LORD, but gracious words are pure.
ESV.27: Whoever is greedy for unjust gain troubles his own household, but he who hates bribes will live.
ESV.28: The heart of the righteous ponders how to answer, but the mouth of the wicked pours out evil things.
ESV.29: The LORD is far from the wicked, but he hears the prayer of the righteous.
ESV.30: The light of the eyes rejoices the heart, and good news refreshes  the bones.
ESV.31: The ear that listens to life-giving reproof will dwell among the wise.
ESV.32: Whoever ignores instruction despises himself, but he who listens to reproof gains intelligence.
ESV.33: The fear of the LORD is instruction in wisdom, and humility comes before honor.

NASB.1: A gentle answer turns away wrath, But a harsh word stirs up anger.
NASB.2: The tongue of the wise makes knowledge acceptable, But the mouth of fools spouts folly.
NASB.3: The eyes of the LORD are in every place, Watching the evil and the good.
NASB.4: A soothing tongue is a tree of life, But perversion in it crushes the spirit.
NASB.5: A fool rejects his father's discipline, But he who regards reproof is sensible.
NASB.6: Great wealth is [in] the house of the righteous, But trouble is in the income of the wicked.
NASB.7: The lips of the wise spread knowledge, But the hearts of fools are not so.
NASB.8: The sacrifice of the wicked is an abomination to the LORD, But the prayer of the upright is His delight.
NASB.9: The way of the wicked is an abomination to the LORD, But He loves one who pursues righteousness.
NASB.10: Grievous punishment is for him who forsakes the way; He who hates reproof will die.
NASB.11: Sheol and Abaddon [lie open] before the LORD, How much more the hearts of men!
NASB.12: A scoffer does not love one who reproves him, He will not go to the wise.
NASB.13: A joyful heart makes a cheerful face, But when the heart is sad, the spirit is broken.
NASB.14: The mind of the intelligent seeks knowledge, But the mouth of fools feeds on folly.
NASB.15: All the days of the afflicted are bad, But a cheerful heart [has] a continual feast.
NASB.16: Better is a little with the fear of the LORD Than great treasure and turmoil with it.
NASB.17: Better is a dish of vegetables where love is Than a fattened ox [served] with hatred.
NASB.18: A hot-tempered man stirs up strife, But the slow to anger calms a dispute.
NASB.19: The way of the lazy is as a hedge of thorns, But the path of the upright is a highway.
NASB.20: A wise son makes a father glad, But a foolish man despises his mother.
NASB.21: Folly is joy to him who lacks sense, But a man of understanding walks straight.
NASB.22: Without consultation, plans are frustrated, But with many counselors they succeed.
NASB.23: A man has joy in an apt answer, And how delightful is a timely word!
NASB.24: The path of life [leads] upward for the wise That he may keep away from Sheol below.
NASB.25: The LORD will tear down the house of the proud, But He will establish the boundary of the widow.
NASB.26: Evil plans are an abomination to the LORD, But pleasant words are pure.
NASB.27: He who profits illicitly troubles his own house, But he who hates bribes will live.
NASB.28: The heart of the righteous ponders how to answer, But the mouth of the wicked pours out evil things.
NASB.29: The LORD is far from the wicked, But He hears the prayer of the righteous.
NASB.30: Bright eyes gladden the heart; Good news puts fat on the bones.
NASB.31: He whose ear listens to the life-giving reproof Will dwell among the wise.
NASB.32: He who neglects discipline despises himself, But he who listens to reproof acquires understanding.
NASB.33: The fear of the LORD is the instruction for wisdom, And before honor [comes] humility.

NLT.1:
A gentle answer deflects anger,
but harsh words make tempers flare.
NLT.2:
The tongue of the wise makes knowledge appealing,
but the mouth of a fool belches out foolishness.
NLT.3:
The LORD is watching everywhere,
keeping his eye on both the evil and the good.
NLT.4:
Gentle words are a tree of life;
a deceitful tongue crushes the spirit.
NLT.5:
Only a fool despises a parent’s discipline;
whoever learns from correction is wise.
NLT.6:
There is treasure in the house of the godly,
but the earnings of the wicked bring trouble.
NLT.7:
The lips of the wise give good advice;
the heart of a fool has none to give.
NLT.8:
The LORD detests the sacrifice of the wicked,
but he delights in the prayers of the upright.
NLT.9:
The LORD detests the way of the wicked,
but he loves those who pursue godliness.
NLT.10:
Whoever abandons the right path will be severely disciplined;
whoever hates correction will die.
NLT.11:
Even Death and Destruction hold no secrets from the LORD.
How much more does he know the human heart!
NLT.12:
Mockers hate to be corrected,
so they stay away from the wise.
NLT.13:
A glad heart makes a happy face;
a broken heart crushes the spirit.
NLT.14:
A wise person is hungry for knowledge,
while the fool feeds on trash.
NLT.15:
For the despondent, every day brings trouble;
for the happy heart, life is a continual feast.
NLT.16:
Better to have little, with fear for the LORD,
than to have great treasure and inner turmoil.
NLT.17:
A bowl of vegetables with someone you love
is better than steak with someone you hate.
NLT.18:
A hot-tempered person starts fights;
a cool-tempered person stops them.
NLT.19:
A lazy person’s way is blocked with briers,
but the path of the upright is an open highway.
NLT.20:
Sensible children bring joy to their father;
foolish children despise their mother.
NLT.21:
Foolishness brings joy to those with no sense;
a sensible person stays on the right path.
NLT.22:
Plans go wrong for lack of advice;
many advisers bring success.
NLT.23:
Everyone enjoys a fitting reply;
it is wonderful to say the right thing at the right time!
NLT.24:
The path of life leads upward for the wise;
they leave the grave behind.
NLT.25:
The LORD tears down the house of the proud,
but he protects the property of widows.
NLT.26:
The LORD detests evil plans,
but he delights in pure words.
NLT.27:
Greed brings grief to the whole family,
but those who hate bribes will live.
NLT.28:
The heart of the godly thinks carefully before speaking;
the mouth of the wicked overflows with evil words.
NLT.29:
The LORD is far from the wicked,
but he hears the prayers of the righteous.
NLT.30:
A cheerful look brings joy to the heart;
good news makes for good health.
NLT.31:
If you listen to constructive criticism,
you will be at home among the wise.
NLT.32:
If you reject discipline, you only harm yourself;
but if you listen to correction, you grow in understanding.
NLT.33:
Fear of the LORD teaches wisdom;
humility precedes honor.

WEB.1: A gentle answer turns away wrath, but a harsh word stirs up anger.
WEB.2: The tongue of the wise commends knowledge, but the mouth of fools gush out folly.
WEB.3: Yahweh’s eyes are everywhere, keeping watch on the evil and the good.
WEB.4: A gentle tongue is a tree of life, but deceit in it crushes the spirit.
WEB.5: A fool despises his father’s correction, but he who heeds reproof shows prudence.
WEB.6: In the house of the righteous is much treasure, but the income of the wicked brings trouble.
WEB.7: The lips of the wise spread knowledge; not so with the heart of fools.
WEB.8: The sacrifice made by the wicked is an abomination to Yahweh, but the prayer of the upright is his delight.
WEB.9: The way of the wicked is an abomination to Yahweh, but he loves him who follows after righteousness.
WEB.10: There is stern discipline for one who forsakes the way: whoever hates reproof shall die.
WEB.11: Sheol  and Abaddon are before Yahweh — how much more then the hearts of the children of men!
WEB.12: A scoffer doesn’t love to be reproved; he will not go to the wise.
WEB.13: A glad heart makes a cheerful face; but an aching heart breaks the spirit.
WEB.14: The heart of one who has understanding seeks knowledge, but the mouths of fools feed on folly.
WEB.15: All the days of the afflicted are wretched, but one who has a cheerful heart enjoys a continual feast.
WEB.16: Better is little, with the fear of Yahweh, than great treasure with trouble.
WEB.17: Better is a dinner of herbs, where love is, than a fattened calf with hatred.
WEB.18: A wrathful man stirs up contention, but one who is slow to anger appeases strife.
WEB.19: The way of the sluggard is like a thorn patch, but the path of the upright is a highway.
WEB.20: A wise son makes a father glad, but a foolish man despises his mother.
WEB.21: Folly is joy to one who is void of wisdom, but a man of understanding keeps his way straight.
WEB.22: Where there is no counsel, plans fail; but in a multitude of counselors they are established.
WEB.23: Joy comes to a man with the reply of his mouth. How good is a word at the right time!
WEB.24: The path of life leads upward for the wise, to keep him from going downward to Sheol.
WEB.25: Yahweh will uproot the house of the proud, but he will keep the widow’s borders intact.
WEB.26: Yahweh detests the thoughts of the wicked, but the thoughts of the pure are pleasing.
WEB.27: He who is greedy for gain troubles his own house, but he who hates bribes will live.
WEB.28: The heart of the righteous weighs answers, but the mouth of the wicked gushes out evil.
WEB.29: Yahweh is far from the wicked, but he hears the prayer of the righteous.
WEB.30: The light of the eyes rejoices the heart. Good news gives health to the bones.
WEB.31: The ear that listens to reproof lives, and will be at home among the wise.
WEB.32: He who refuses correction despises his own soul, but he who listens to reproof gets understanding.
WEB.33: The fear of Yahweh teaches wisdom. Before honor is humility.

Plans fail when there is no counsel,
but with many advisers they succeed. ⓕ
CSB17.23:
A person takes joy in giving an answer; [2]
and a timely word — how good that is! ⓖ
CSB17.24:
For the prudent the path of life leads upward,
so that he may avoid going down to Sheol.
CSB17.25:
The LORD tears apart the house of the proud,
but he protects the widow’s territory. ⓗ
CSB17.26:
The LORD detests the plans of the one who is evil,
but pleasant words are pure. ⓘ
CSB17.27:
The one who profits dishonestly ⓙ troubles his household,
but the one who hates bribes will live. ⓚ
CSB17.28:
The mind of the righteous person thinks before answering, ⓛ
but the mouth of the wicked blurts out evil things. ⓜ
CSB17.29:
The LORD is far from the wicked, ⓝ
but he hears the prayer of the righteous. ⓐ
CSB17.30:
Bright eyes cheer the heart;
good news strengthens [3] the bones. ⓑ
CSB17.31:
One who [4] listens to life-giving rebukes ⓒ
will be at home among the wise.
CSB17.32:
Anyone who ignores discipline despises himself, ⓓ
but whoever listens to correction acquires good sense. [5] ⓔ
CSB17.33:
The fear of the LORD is what wisdom teaches,
and humility comes before honor. ⓕ

AMP.1:
A soft and gentle and thoughtful answer turns away wrath,
But harsh and painful and careless words stir up anger.
AMP.2:
The tongue of the wise speaks knowledge that is pleasing and acceptable,
But the [babbling] mouth of fools spouts folly.
AMP.3:
The eyes of the LORD are in every place,
Watching the evil and the good [in all their endeavors].
AMP.4:
A soothing tongue [speaking words that build up and encourage] is a tree of life,
But a perversive tongue [speaking words that overwhelm and depress] crushes the spirit.
AMP.5:
A [flippant, arrogant] fool rejects his father's instruction and correction,
But he who [is willing to learn and] regards and keeps in mind a reprimand acquires good sense.
AMP.6:
Great and priceless treasure is in the house of the [consistently] righteous one [who seeks godly instruction and grows in wisdom],
But trouble is in the income of the wicked one [who rejects the laws of God].
AMP.7:
The lips of the wise spread knowledge [sifting it as chaff from the grain];
But the hearts of [shortsighted] fools are not so.
AMP.8:
The sacrifice of the wicked is hateful and exceedingly offensive to the LORD,
But the prayer of the upright is His delight!
AMP.9:
The way [of life] of the wicked is hateful and exceedingly offensive to the LORD,
But He loves one who pursues righteousness [personal integrity, moral courage and honorable character].
AMP.10:
There is severe discipline for him who turns from the way [of righteousness];
And he who hates correction will die.
AMP.11:
Sheol (the nether world, the place of the dead) and Abaddon (the abyss, the place of eternal punishment) lie open before the LORD—
How much more the hearts and inner motives of the children of men.
AMP.12:
A scoffer [unlike a wise man] resents one who rebukes him and tries to teach him;
Nor will he go to the wise [for counsel and instruction].
AMP.13:
A heart full of joy and goodness makes a cheerful face,
But when a heart is full of sadness the spirit is crushed.
AMP.14:
The mind of the intelligent and discerning seeks knowledge and eagerly inquires after it,
But the mouth of the [stubborn] fool feeds on foolishness.
AMP.15:
All the days of the afflicted are bad,
But a glad heart has a continual feast [regardless of the circumstances].
AMP.16:
Better is a little with the [reverent, worshipful] fear of the LORD
Than great treasure and trouble with it.
AMP.17:
Better is a dinner of vegetables and herbs where love is present
Than a fattened ox served with hatred.
AMP.18:
A hot-tempered man stirs up strife,
But he who is slow to anger and patient calms disputes.
AMP.19:
The way of the lazy is like a hedge of thorns [it pricks, lacerates, and entangles him],
But the way [of life] of the upright is smooth and open like a highway.
AMP.20:
A wise son makes a father glad,
But a foolish man despises his mother.
AMP.21:
Foolishness is joy to him who is without heart and lacks [intelligent, common] sense,
But a man of understanding walks uprightly [making his course straight].
AMP.22:
Without consultation and wise advice, plans are frustrated,
But with many counselors they are established and succeed.
AMP.23:
A man has joy in giving an appropriate answer,
And how good and delightful is a word spoken at the right moment—how good it is!
AMP.24:
The [chosen] path of life leads upward for the wise,
That he may keep away from Sheol (the nether world, the place of the dead) below.
AMP.25:
The LORD will tear down the house of the proud and arrogant (self-righteous),
But He will establish and protect the boundaries [of the land] of the [godly] widow.
AMP.26:
Evil plans and thoughts of the wicked are exceedingly vile and offensive to the LORD,
But pure words are pleasant words to Him.
AMP.27:
He who profits unlawfully brings suffering to his own house,
But he who hates bribes [and does not receive nor pay them] will live.
AMP.28:
The heart of the righteous thinks carefully about how to answer [in a wise and appropriate and timely way],
But the [babbling] mouth of the wicked pours out malevolent things.
AMP.29:
The LORD is far from the wicked [and distances Himself from them],
But He hears the prayer of the [consistently] righteous [that is, those with spiritual integrity and moral courage].
AMP.30:
The light of the eyes rejoices the hearts of others,
And good news puts fat on the bones.
AMP.31:
The ear that listens to and learns from the life-giving rebuke (reprimand, censure)
Will remain among the wise.
AMP.32:
He who neglects and ignores instruction and discipline despises himself,
But he who learns from rebuke acquires understanding [and grows in wisdom].
AMP.33:
The [reverent] fear of the LORD [that is, worshiping Him and regarding Him as truly awesome] is the instruction for wisdom [its starting point and its essence];
And before honor comes humility.

MSG.1:
A gentle response defuses anger,
but a sharp tongue kindles a temper-fire.
MSG.2:
Knowledge flows like spring water from the wise;
fools are leaky faucets, dripping nonsense.
MSG.3:
God doesn't miss a thing—
he's alert to good and evil alike.
MSG.4:
Kind words heal and help;
cutting words wound and maim.
MSG.5:
Moral dropouts won't listen to their elders;
welcoming correction is a mark of good sense.
MSG.6:
The lives of God-loyal people flourish;
a misspent life is soon bankrupt.
MSG.7:
Perceptive words spread knowledge;
fools are hollow—there's nothing to them.
MSG.8:
God can't stand pious poses,
but he delights in genuine prayers.
MSG.9:
A life frittered away disgusts God;
he loves those who run straight for the finish line.
MSG.10:
It's a school of hard knocks for those who leave God's path,
a dead-end street for those who hate God's rules.
MSG.11:
Even hell holds no secrets from God—
do you think he can't read human hearts?
MSG.12:
Know-it-alls don't like being told what to do;
they avoid the company of wise men and women.
MSG.13:
A cheerful heart brings a smile to your face;
a sad heart makes it hard to get through the day.
MSG.14:
An intelligent person is always eager to take in more truth;
fools feed on fast-food fads and fancies.
MSG.15:
A miserable heart means a miserable life;
a cheerful heart fills the day with song.
MSG.16:
A simple life in the Fear-of-God
is better than a rich life with a ton of headaches.
MSG.17:
Better a bread crust shared in love
than a slab of prime rib served in hate.
MSG.18:
Hot tempers start fights;
a calm, cool spirit keeps the peace.
MSG.19:
The path of lazy people is overgrown with briers;
the diligent walk down a smooth road.
MSG.20:
Intelligent children make their parents proud;
lazy students embarrass their parents.
MSG.21:
The empty-headed treat life as a plaything;
the perceptive grasp its meaning and make a go of it.
MSG.22:
Refuse good advice and watch your plans fail;
take good counsel and watch them succeed.
MSG.23:
Congenial conversation—what a pleasure!
The right word at the right time—beautiful!
MSG.24:
Life ascends to the heights for the thoughtful—
it's a clean about-face from descent into hell.
MSG.25:
God smashes the pretensions of the arrogant;
he stands with those who have no standing.
MSG.26:
God can't stand evil scheming,
but he puts words of grace and beauty on display.
MSG.27:
A greedy and grasping person destroys community;
those who refuse to exploit live and let live.
MSG.28:
Prayerful answers come from God-loyal people;
the wicked are sewers of abuse.
MSG.29:
God keeps his distance from the wicked;
he closely attends to the prayers of God-loyal people.
MSG.30:
A twinkle in the eye means joy in the heart,
and good news makes you feel fit as a fiddle.
MSG.31:
Listen to good advice if you want to live well,
an honored guest among wise men and women.
MSG.32:
An undisciplined, self-willed life is puny;
an obedient, God-willed life is spacious.
MSG.33:
Fear-of-God is a school in skilled living—
first you learn humility, then you experience glory.

NIV.1:
A gentle answer turns away wrath,
but a harsh word stirs up anger.
NIV.2:
The tongue of the wise commends knowledge,
but the mouth of the fool gushes folly.
NIV.3:
The eyes of the Lord are everywhere,
keeping watch on the wicked and the good.
NIV.4:
The tongue that brings healing is a tree of life,
but a deceitful tongue crushes the spirit.
NIV.5:
A fool spurns his father’s discipline,
but whoever heeds correction shows prudence.
NIV.6:
The house of the righteous contains great treasure,
but the income of the wicked brings them trouble.
NIV.7:
The lips of the wise spread knowledge;
not so the hearts of fools.
NIV.8:
The Lord detests the sacrifice of the wicked,
but the prayer of the upright pleases him.
NIV.9:
The Lord detests the way of the wicked
but he loves those who pursue righteousness.
NIV.10:
Stern discipline awaits him who leaves the path;
he who hates correction will die.
NIV.11:
Death and Destruction lie open before the Lord—
how much more the hearts of men!
NIV.12:
A mocker resents correction;
he will not consult the wise.
NIV.13:
A happy heart makes the face cheerful,
but heartache crushes the spirit.
NIV.14:
The discerning heart seeks knowledge,
but the mouth of a fool feeds on folly.
NIV.15:
All the days of the oppressed are wretched,
but the cheerful heart has a continual feast.
NIV.16:
Better a little with the fear of the Lord
than great wealth with turmoil.
NIV.17:
Better a meal of vegetables where there is love
than a fattened calf with hatred.
NIV.18:
A hot-tempered man stirs up dissension,
but a patient man calms a quarrel.
NIV.19:
The way of the sluggard is blocked with thorns,
but the path of the upright is a highway.
NIV.20:
A wise son brings joy to his father,
but a foolish man despises his mother.
NIV.21:
Folly delights a man who lacks judgment,
but a man of understanding keeps a straight course.
NIV.22:
Plans fail for lack of counsel,
but with many advisers they succeed.
NIV.23:
A man finds joy in giving an apt reply—
and how good is a timely word!
NIV.24:
The path of life leads upward for the wise
to keep him from going down to the grave.
NIV.25:
The Lord tears down the proud man’s house
but he keeps the widow’s boundaries intact.
NIV.26:
The Lord detests the thoughts of the wicked,
but those of the pure are pleasing to him.
NIV.27:
A greedy man brings trouble to his family,
but he who hates bribes will live.
NIV.28:
The heart of the righteous weighs its answers,
but the mouth of the wicked gushes evil.
NIV.29:
The Lord is far from the wicked
but he hears the prayer of the righteous.
NIV.30:
A cheerful look brings joy to the heart,
and good news gives health to the bones.
NIV.31:
He who listens to a life-giving rebuke
will be at home among the wise.
NIV.32:
He who ignores discipline despises himself,
but whoever heeds correction gains understanding.
NIV.33:
The fear of the Lord teaches a man wisdom,
and humility comes before honor.

NRSVCE.1:
A soft answer turns away wrath,
but a harsh word stirs up anger.
NRSVCE.2:
The tongue of the wise dispenses knowledge,
but the mouths of fools pour out folly.
NRSVCE.3:
The eyes of the LORD are in every place,
keeping watch on the evil and the good.
NRSVCE.4:
A gentle tongue is a tree of life,
but perverseness in it breaks the spirit.
NRSVCE.5:
A fool despises a parent’s instruction,
but the one who heeds admonition is prudent.
NRSVCE.6:
In the house of the righteous there is much treasure,
but trouble befalls the income of the wicked.
NRSVCE.7:
The lips of the wise spread knowledge;
not so the minds of fools.
NRSVCE.8:
The sacrifice of the wicked is an abomination to the LORD,
but the prayer of the upright is his delight.
NRSVCE.9:
The way of the wicked is an abomination to the LORD,
but he loves the one who pursues righteousness.
NRSVCE.10:
There is severe discipline for one who forsakes the way,
but one who hates a rebuke will die.
NRSVCE.11:
Sheol and Abaddon lie open before the LORD,
how much more human hearts!
NRSVCE.12:
Scoffers do not like to be rebuked;
they will not go to the wise.
NRSVCE.13:
A glad heart makes a cheerful countenance,
but by sorrow of heart the spirit is broken.
NRSVCE.14:
The mind of one who has understanding seeks knowledge,
but the mouths of fools feed on folly.
NRSVCE.15:
All the days of the poor are hard,
but a cheerful heart has a continual feast.
NRSVCE.16:
Better is a little with the fear of the LORD
than great treasure and trouble with it.
NRSVCE.17:
Better is a dinner of vegetables where love is
than a fatted ox and hatred with it.
NRSVCE.18:
Those who are hot-tempered stir up strife,
but those who are slow to anger calm contention.
NRSVCE.19:
The way of the lazy is overgrown with thorns,
but the path of the upright is a level highway.
NRSVCE.20:
A wise child makes a glad father,
but the foolish despise their mothers.
NRSVCE.21:
Folly is a joy to one who has no sense,
but a person of understanding walks straight ahead.
NRSVCE.22:
Without counsel, plans go wrong,
but with many advisers they succeed.
NRSVCE.23:
To make an apt answer is a joy to anyone,
and a word in season, how good it is!
NRSVCE.24:
For the wise the path of life leads upward,
in order to avoid Sheol below.
NRSVCE.25:
The LORD tears down the house of the proud,
but maintains the widow’s boundaries.
NRSVCE.26:
Evil plans are an abomination to the LORD,
but gracious words are pure.
NRSVCE.27:
Those who are greedy for unjust gain make trouble for their households,
but those who hate bribes will live.
NRSVCE.28:
The mind of the righteous ponders how to answer,
but the mouth of the wicked pours out evil.
NRSVCE.29:
The LORD is far from the wicked,
but he hears the prayer of the righteous.
NRSVCE.30:
The light of the eyes rejoices the heart,
and good news refreshes the body.
NRSVCE.31:
The ear that heeds wholesome admonition
will lodge among the wise.
NRSVCE.32:
Those who ignore instruction despise themselves,
but those who heed admonition gain understanding.
NRSVCE.33:
The fear of the LORD is instruction in wisdom,
and humility goes before honor.

NET.1: A gentle response turns away anger, but a harsh word stirs up wrath.
NET.2: The tongue of the wise treats knowledge correctly, but the mouth of the fool spouts out folly.
NET.3: The eyes of the LORD are in every place, keeping watch on those who are evil and those who are good.
NET.4: Speech that heals is like a life-giving tree, but a perverse tongue breaks the spirit.
NET.5: A fool rejects his father’s discipline, but whoever heeds reproof shows good sense.
NET.6: In the house of the righteous is abundant wealth, but the income of the wicked brings trouble.
NET.7: The lips of the wise spread knowledge, but not so the heart of fools.
NET.8: The LORD abhors the sacrifices of the wicked, but the prayer of the upright pleases him.
NET.9: The LORD abhors the way of the wicked, but he loves those who pursue righteousness.
NET.10: Severe discipline is for the one who abandons the way; the one who hates reproof will die.
NET.11: Death and Destruction are before the LORD — how much more the hearts of humans!
NET.12: The scorner does not love one who corrects him; he will not go to the wise.
NET.13: A joyful heart makes the face cheerful, but by a painful heart the spirit is broken.
NET.14: The discerning heart seeks knowledge, but the mouth of fools feeds on folly.
NET.15: All the days of the afflicted are bad, but one with a cheerful heart has a continual feast.
NET.16: Better is little with the fear of the LORD than great wealth and turmoil with it.
NET.17: Better a meal of vegetables where there is love than a fattened ox where there is hatred.
NET.18: A quick-tempered person stirs up dissension, but one who is slow to anger calms a quarrel.
NET.19: The way of the sluggard is like a hedge of thorns, but the path of the upright is like a highway.
NET.20: A wise child brings joy to his father, but a foolish person despises his mother.
NET.21: Folly is a joy to one who lacks sense, but one who has understanding follows an upright course.
NET.22: Plans fail when there is no counsel, but with abundant advisers they are established.
NET.23: A person has joy in giving an appropriate answer, and a word at the right time — how good it is!
NET.24: The path of life is upward for the wise person, to keep him from going downward to Sheol.
NET.25: The LORD tears down the house of the proud, but he maintains the boundaries of the widow.
NET.26: The LORD abhors the plans of the wicked, but pleasant words are pure.
NET.27: The one who is greedy for gain troubles his household, but whoever hates bribes will live.
NET.28: The heart of the righteous considers how to answer, but the mouth of the wicked pours out evil things.
NET.29: The LORD is far from the wicked, but he hears the prayer of the righteous.
NET.30: A bright look brings joy to the heart, and good news gives health to the body.
NET.31: The person who hears the reproof that leads to life is at home among the wise.
NET.32: The one who refuses correction despises himself, but whoever hears reproof acquires understanding.
NET.33: The fear of the LORD provides wise instruction, and before honor comes humility.
